requirement.original: 重构 auto-commit/checkpoint 系统，让 git blame 的每一行都指向真实的语义 commit —— 所有自动化快照改写到 refs/checkpoints/<sanitized-branch>，不再污染任何分支 HEAD。完整 v2 patch：7 个文件 + CAS 并发保护 + 边界守卫 + 文档。
requirement.clarified: Refactor the auto-commit/checkpoint system: extract a shared checkpoint-core.sh library that uses git commit-tree + CAS git update-ref to write all automated snapshots to refs/checkpoints/<sanitized-branch> instead of HEAD. Convert all 7 existing hook files to use the shared library. Add CAS concurrency protection, edge-case guards (empty repo, detached HEAD, branch slash sanitization), defensive temp-index cleanup, rate-limited background ref push, and update documentation.
requirement.what: Replace 7 direct `git commit` calls in hooks with calls to a shared checkpoint-core.sh library that writes to refs/checkpoints/<branch> via git plumbing (commit-tree + update-ref CAS), never touching HEAD.
requirement.why: Automated snapshot commits pollute branch HEAD, making `git log`, `git blame`, and PR history unreadable. Semantic commits authored by developers are buried among auto-commits. Moving snapshots to a dedicated ref namespace solves this without losing snapshot history.

included: New shared library checkpoint-core.sh with write_checkpoint function
Conversion of all 7 hook files from git commit to lib calls
CAS retry loop (5 attempts) with checkpoint.log failure logging
GIT_INDEX_FILE isolation for temp tree building
Defensive sweep of stale temp index files (>60 min)
Edge cases: empty repo, detached HEAD, branch slash in name
push.sh dirty-tree guard with 'commit first' error
Rate-limited background push of checkpoint ref (30s per repo)
excluded: Retroactive cleanup of existing checkpoint commits on master
Retention or pruning policy for refs/checkpoints/* growth

success_criteria: git log HEAD shows zero auto-generated snapshot commits after implementation
git log refs/checkpoints/master shows all automated snapshots in linear chain
All 8 acceptance tests pass (see acceptance_tests field)
git fsck --unreachable shows no dangling commits from concurrent writes
/push on dirty tree exits non-zero with 'commit first' message
constraints: Must not use git commit anywhere in checkpoint-core.sh or converted callers
Push must not use -f flag (CAS guarantees fast-forward chain)
Must not modify user's real .git/index (use GIT_INDEX_FILE isolation)
Temp index files must be cleaned up via trap EXIT INT TERM HUP
Logs must be append-only to ~/.claude/logs/checkpoint.log and checkpoint-push.log

root_cause_analysis.symptom: git log and git blame on any branch show a mixture of semantic commits and auto-generated snapshot commits (messages: 'Auto-commit: ...', 'checkpoint: Auto-save at ...'), making history unreadable and git blame unreliable.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: All 7 hook files call `git commit` directly against the current working branch HEAD. There is no separation between snapshot commits and semantic commits — both land in the same ref chain.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: f5241eb — checkpoint: Auto-save at 2026-04-15 21:08:34 (representative example; the pattern predates this commit)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Original hooks were built to ensure no work is lost by checkpointing frequently. Using `git commit` was the simplest approach at that time.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: As the workflow matured and the repo became a shared development reference, clean semantic history became important. `git blame` now shows auto-commit authors for most lines. PR diffs include dozens of noisy auto-commit entries.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Pattern established from initial hook authoring; f5241eb (2026-04-15) and b260606 (2026-04-16) are recent examples of HEAD pollution.

setup.data_state: Active git repository with staged and unstaged changes present

evidence.observed: git log and git blame on any branch show a mixture of semantic commits and auto-generated snapshot commits making history unreadable

measured.value: 6 of last 8 commits on master are auto-generated: 'Auto-commit: 2026-04-16 06:38:09', 'Auto-commit: 2026-04-16 06:29:36', 'Auto-commit: 2026-04-16 06:03:37', 'checkpoint: Auto-save at 2026-04-15 21:08:34', 'checkpoint: Before cleanup...', 'checkpoint: Auto-save at 2026-01-11 19:55:07'

expected.value: git log HEAD shows only semantic commits (e.g., 'feat(orchestrator): ...', 'fix(hooks): ...'); automated snapshots visible only via git log refs/checkpoints/master
expected.source: User requirement verbatim; git plumbing documentation for commit-tree + update-ref pattern

evidence.gap: 7 hook files write 100% of snapshots to HEAD; 0 files currently write to refs/checkpoints/*. Every automated save pollutes HEAD.

reference_source.description: QA subagent pre-investigation findings (scope, algorithm pseudocode, edge cases) verified by direct file reads and grep in this BA session. Algorithm uses standard git plumbing commands (commit-tree, update-ref, read-tree, write-tree) which are tier_1_external by nature (git documentation), accessed via tier_2_verified pre-investigation.
reference_source.url_or_location: Pre-investigation block in BA prompt; cross-verified against /root/.claude/hooks/*.sh live file reads at /root/.claude/hooks/

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts exist. This is the initial v2 design targeting L4 (logic layer: replacing git commit with git plumbing commit-tree + update-ref CAS, changing the data flow of where commits are written).

dependencies.git_version: standard git with plumbing commands commit-tree, write-tree, read-tree, update-ref
dependencies.log_directory: ~/.claude/logs/ (must exist or be created by lib)

environment.git_root: varies per invocation; detected from GIT_CHECKOUT_DIR or cwd

development_approach.strategy: 1) Create ~/.claude/hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh with write_checkpoint bash function implementing git plumbing chain (read-tree → add -A → write-tree → commit-tree → update-ref CAS with 5-retry). 2) Convert posttool-git-checkpoint.sh, auto-commit.sh, stop-git-commit.sh, checkpoint.sh, git-fswatch.sh to source lib and call write_checkpoint. 3) Modify push.sh to remove auto-commit block and add dirty-tree fail-fast guard. 4) Delete smart-checkpoint.sh (confirmed dormant duplicate; dev must verify not in settings.json first). 5) Update CLAUDE.md documentation section.
development_approach.validation_approach: Run 8 acceptance tests in isolated scratch git repos. Test AC6 (10 parallel invocations) in a subshell loop. Verify git fsck after AC6. Check git log HEAD unchanged after AC1.

acceptance_tests.name: No HEAD pollution
acceptance_tests.description: Create 15 files in scratch repo -> git log HEAD unchanged; git log refs/checkpoints/master has 1 commit.

acceptance_tests.name: Full working-tree capture
acceptance_tests.description: Modify + delete + chmod -> all captured in next checkpoint tree.

acceptance_tests.name: Empty repo bootstrap
acceptance_tests.description: Empty repo (no commits) -> first checkpoint succeeds as root commit on ref.

acceptance_tests.name: Detached HEAD fallback
acceptance_tests.description: git checkout --detach then edit -> checkpoint on refs/checkpoints/detached-<sha>.

acceptance_tests.name: Branch slash sanitization
acceptance_tests.description: Branch 'feature/x' -> checkpoint on refs/checkpoints/feature-x (no D/F conflict).

acceptance_tests.name: Concurrent invocations
acceptance_tests.description: 10 parallel invocations of write_checkpoint -> ref has exactly 10 linear commits; git fsck --unreachable shows no new dangling commits.

acceptance_tests.name: Push dirty-tree guard
acceptance_tests.description: /push in clean tree succeeds; /push in dirty tree fails with 'commit first' message.

acceptance_tests.name: Stop hook idempotency
acceptance_tests.description: Stop hook chain fires -> at most ONE new commit on ref (no double from stop-git-commit + auto-commit both running when tree is unchanged).

research_findings.reason: Straightforward refactoring of known shell scripts using well-documented git plumbing commands (commit-tree, update-ref, read-tree, write-tree). Algorithm is fully specified in pre-investigation. No architectural ambiguity requiring industry research.
